llm_response:
The journey begins in the first month with a focus on securing the ideal venue, as this decision sets the tone for the entire celebration. Couples must finalize their location early to ensure availability, considering factors like capacity and style. Once the venue is locked in, the foundation for the event is established, allowing the team to move forward with confidence.

In the second month, attention shifts to refining the guest list based on the confirmed venue capacity and personal preferences. Attendees are identified and invitations are drafted, ensuring that the number of people aligns with the space. This phase also involves creating a preliminary schedule for the day, which will be adjusted as more details emerge.

By the third month, the focus turns to selecting key vendors such as photographers, caterers, and florists to complement the chosen venue. Contracts are signed, and initial deposits are made to secure these essential services. Communication channels are opened with each vendor to discuss their vision and how they will integrate with the overall theme.

The fourth month is dedicated to finalizing the guest list details and sending out formal invitations. RSVPs are tracked closely to confirm attendance numbers, which may lead to minor adjustments in catering or seating arrangements. A detailed run-of-show is created, outlining every moment of the ceremony and reception.

In the fifth month, all vendor coordination intensifies as final designs are approved and logistics are confirmed. The guest list is finalized, and any last-minute changes to the schedule are addressed. A comprehensive checklist is compiled to ensure nothing is overlooked before the big day arrives.

The final month before the wedding is reserved for final walkthroughs at the venue and a last review of all vendor contracts. The guest list is confirmed one last time, and all invitations are mailed or delivered. The couple and their planners prepare mentally and physically, ensuring everything is ready for the celebration.
